texto: Because of the earth’s roundness, Genghis Khan, in the fever of possession and destruction, hastened his own overthrow by invading lands that he had already razed and conquered. Not only is it impossible to know from where we come, but also from whom we come: nothing in common, in any case, with those who pass for being the “authors of our days” – which days? Better to invent a genealogy based on pure whim and the leanings of our hearts, but what if they don’t agree?
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de André Breton que provoca limites e escolhas conscientes. Ao questionar “Because of the earth’s roundness, Genghis Khan, in the fever of possession and destruction, haste…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre amor, creativity, literature, com foco em literature e writers.

Better to invent a genealogy based on pure whim and the leanings of our hearts, but what if they don’t agree?

frase_original: It is hard not to see into the future, faced with today's blind architecture - a thousand times more stupid and more revolting than that of other ages. How bored we shall be inside!

texto: We are in front of a fountain, whose jet she seems to be watching. 'Those are your thoughts and mine. Look where they all start from, how high they reach, and then how it's still prettier when they fall back. And then they dissolve immediately, driven back up with the same strength, then there's that broken spurt again, that fall ... and so on indefinitely.
explicacao: Citação de André Breton sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “We are in front of a fountain, whose jet she seems to be watching. 'Those are your thoughts and m…” articula reflexao, surrealism, thought em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como thought.

texto: We all love conflagrations. When the sky changes color, it is a dead man's passing.
explicacao: Citação de André Breton sobre vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. “We all love conflagrations. When the sky changes color, it is a dead man's passing.” articula amor, death, surrealism em registro de amor, com relevância para contextos como luto e mudanca.

texto: I have always been amazed at the way an ordinary observer lends so much more credence and attaches so much more importance to waking events than to those occurring in dreams... Man... is above all the plaything of his memory.
explicacao: Citação de Andre Breton sobre temas ligados a motivacao. “I have always been amazed at the way an ordinary observer lends so much more credence and attache…” articula motivacao, man, memory em registro de motivacao, com relevância para contextos como termino e memory.

texto: I am concerned with facts of quite unverifiable intrinsic value, but which, by their absolutely unexpected violently fortuitous character, and the kind of associations of suspect ideas they provoke.
